name: Will Lee
abstract: Will Lee (born September 8, 1952) is an American jazz fusion bassist known for his work on the Late Show with David Letterman as part of the CBS Orchestra and Paul Shaffer and the World's Most Dangerous Band during Letterman’s tenure as host of NBC's Late Night with David Letterman. Lee has recorded and toured with many artists.

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Brecker
about_quotes.evidence: we had rehearsals that a lot of the guys from the band Dreams — Will Lee on bass... We’d rehearse once or twice a week... And we were a great band... It was really a band of brothers
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Brecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 12, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Malone
about_quotes.evidence: we had a remarkable rhythm section on that show, you know Anton Fig on drums, Will Lee on bass
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Malone interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Moore
about_quotes.evidence: when Jaco Pastorious showed up playing electric bass, and Will Lee and a couple of other guys who were really into it, I said well I never liked practicing the instrument.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Moore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Erskine
about_quotes.evidence: I think Will Lee and I had for Don, we’d call him Uncle Don. And Don had written a tune that we used to play in Steps, which later became known as Steps Ahead
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Erskine interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Orleans, Louisiana, January 9, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: I did some recordings with him. ... Paul Shaffer was the leader and Will Lee playing bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Shelly Berg
about_quotes.evidence: You can put on your Will Lee bass loop and your Peter Erskine drum loop
about_quotes.doc_title: Shelly Berg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000
